Scenario.title: The Outer Space Campaign: Energy Sector Compromise
Scenario.overview: Energy Simple The Outer Space campaign targets the energy sector using spear-phishing techniques to gain initial access, followed by PowerShell execution and data exfiltration.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shared DB or directory, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Phishing, Web, Browser-based attack, Credential compromise

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2010-1297 [CWE-22]: Microsoft Office Word Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.description: The Outer Space campaign sends targeted emails to employees at energy organizations, containing malicious attachments exploiting the CVE-2010-1297 vulnerability in Microsoft Office Word.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2020-5849 [CWE-94]: Microsoft Windows SMBv3 Server Service Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 579 - Replace Winlogon Helper DLL
stage.description: Upon successful exploitation of the attachment, PowerShell is used to download and execute payloads, enabling the attackers to establish persistence and move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 24 - Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate sensitive information from the compromised energy organization's systems, leveraging their understanding of the shared database structures and directory permissions.

Scenario.summary: The Outer Space campaign demonstrates a simple yet effective approach to compromising energy organizations, utilizing spear-phishing, PowerShell, and data exfiltration techniques.
